title: Prosecutor v. Radislav Krstić, Trial Judgment
author_or_org: International Criminal Tribunal for the former Yugoslavia
geographic_scope: Srebrenica enclave, Potočari, Bratunac-Konjević Polje-Nova Kasaba-Zvornik corridor
strengths: Judicially tested factual record with explicit counts for Potočari refugees, breakout-column size, one-third-soldier share, timing, and transfer/execution sequence.
limitations: Not a purpose-built casualty registry; legal language focuses on men of military age regardless of civilian or military status; does not provide a clean soldier-versus-civilian death count.
notes: Key usable excerpts include: approximately 20,000-25,000 refugees at Potočari; 10,000-15,000 men in the column; around one third soldiers; 7,000-8,000 men of military age systematically massacred while about 25,000 others were forcibly transferred.

title: Srebrenica Genocide
strengths: Authoritative missing-person and identification institution; explicit about men and boys killed and Bosnian Serb responsibility.
limitations: Does not separate soldiers from civilians and is not an exposure source.
notes: Useful for corroborating total death magnitude ('more than 8,000') and side attribution.

title: List of Victims
strengths: Named-victim memorial list with a transparent headline count.
limitations: No soldier/civilian split or denominator information; memorial compilation rather than adjudicated registry documentation.
notes: Useful as a high-end named-victim count (8,372, mostly men and boys).

citation_short: Tokača, Bosnian Book of the Dead (2012), Podrinje/Graph 73
title: Bosanska knjiga mrtvih / The Bosnian Book of the Dead: Human Losses in Bosnia and Herzegovina 1991-1995
author_or_org: Mirsad Tokača / Research and Documentation Center Sarajevo
pages_or_sections: Tom I, Podrinje section, Graph 73 (claim recovered via accessible preview/mirror)
geographic_scope: Podrinje municipalities tied to the fall of Srebrenica
strengths: Only located source in this pass that exposes both a total Srebrenica-fall death count and a soldier count for the same narrow period.
limitations: Status coding is operational 'soldier' versus 'civilian' rather than combat/noncombat; source access in this environment relied on preview lines/mirror text rather than a directly fetched page image.
notes: Core claim: 8,331 persons killed from 11-31 July 1995 in the Srebrenica-fall window, of whom 1,416 were soldiers.

citation_short: HRDAG audit of BBD status fields (2007)
title: Evaluation of the Database of the Bosnian Book of the Dead
author_or_org: Marek Szczygiel, Patrick Ball, and Ewa Tabeau / Human Rights Data Analysis Group
date_scope: Review of BBD status coding and completeness
strengths: Directly audits the exact database family used for the soldier/civilian split and explains why status coding may be biased.
limitations: Not episode-specific and not itself a casualty counter.
notes: Essential caveat source: warns that 'Status in War' over-reports soldiers and under-reports civilians, and that combat/noncombat fields are too incomplete for analysis.

citation_short: USHMM Holocaust Encyclopedia, Warsaw Ghetto Uprising
title: Warsaw Ghetto Uprising
strengths: High-quality synthesis giving remaining ghetto population, fighter strength, and event duration.
limitations: Does not cleanly separate fighter deaths from civilian deaths within the liquidation period.

title: Warsaw Ghetto Uprising
strengths: Compact statement of residual ghetto population, fighter numbers, and Stroop's destruction total.
limitations: Short summary rather than a full methodological casualty study; still lacks fighter-death separation.

title: Report to SS officials on the destruction of the Warsaw ghetto, including daily operational reports and a photographic report
author_or_org: Harvard Law School Library Nuremberg Project
strengths: Provides original-report totals often cited in the historiography.
limitations: Perpetrator reporting with obvious bias problems; not a standalone casualty source for DR publication.
notes: Used only to show why the Warsaw episode still needs careful status separation before publication.
